backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 17 to 21 to target the new JDK.
backend/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile and run against Java 17 semantics, failing to utilize Java 21 features or enforce Java 21 compatibility.

backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated maven-compiler-plugin source/target configuration with the modern release flag.
backend/pom.xml.why_exists: The source and target options are deprecated in newer compiler plugin versions and do not enforce bytecode compatibility as strictly as the release flag; using release is required for proper Java 21 module system and API enforcement.

backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade JaCoCo Maven plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
backend/pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of JaCoCo (like 0.8.10) do not support the class file version introduced in Java 21, leading to runtime errors or instrumentation failures during test execution.
